activeGoal: 重构 Sidepanel 会话视图投影与渲染机制以消除流式截断、幽灵用户消息和卡顿问题
designSummary: Full-model refactor approved for Sidepanel transcript projection and renderer. Within ELM-COMP-EXT-SIDEPANEL, redesign the conversation projection as a state-machine-driven Assistant Message Node model that decouples accepted SSE events from UI transcript grouping, keeps reasoning/tool/text inside one stable assistant node until terminal state, prevents synthetic User node creation from stream-status changes or empty payloads, and introduces an incremental renderer contract that updates only the active assistant tail while preserving historical node identity and avoiding whole-list rerender or full markdown reparse on each token.
designDecisions: ADR: Brownfield fit decision. Extend existing software unit ELM-COMP-EXT-SIDEPANEL instead of introducing a new module because reasoningTimeline.ts, reasoningTimelineView.tsx, useSidepanelController.ts, and their tests already form the ownership seam for transcript projection, controller orchestration, and transcript rendering. Rejected python_adapter/app and backend/src because they emit or transport events but do not own UI bubble identity or React render behavior.

ADR: Replace heuristic post-hoc transcript normalization with explicit Assistant Message Node lifecycle rules. Transcript grouping must be derived from stable logical assistant identity and terminal-state boundaries, not from adjacency, anchor drift, or text-similarity coalescing alone, because those heuristics cause assistant truncation and ghost user bubbles during tool/text state changes.

ADR: Renderer contract adopts structural patch vs tail patch semantics. Sealed messages must retain reference stability, while high-frequency assistant deltas update only the active tail through buffered incremental rendering. Renderer must not reparse markdown for the entire transcript or rerender the entire React list on each token.

ADR: Full-model architecture update adds formal behavior/service contracts for ELM-APP-EXT-RUN-CONVERSATION-MAPPER, ELM-APP-EXT-CONVERSATION-RENDERER, and ELM-FUNC-EXT-RENDER-INCREMENTAL-TRANSCRIPT so Sidepanel controller, projection, and rendering responsibilities are separately traceable in the graph. This was required because code markers existed for renderer/mapper seams but the graph lacked explicit architect-managed contract elements for the renderer path.

issues.details: Resolved by successful rework commit 90dc29db55ce82d09cee2a7cb96e488bf7e2433b after scanner-verifiable ArchitectureID coverage was corrected and full-model QA/Audit passed for TASK-014, TASK-015, and TASK-016.

qa.summary: QA passed for commit 90dc29db55ce82d09cee2a7cb96e488bf7e2433b covering TASK-014, TASK-015, TASK-016. Verified ApplicationFunction contracts ELM-FUNC-EXT-PROJECT-TRANSCRIPT and ELM-FUNC-EXT-RENDER-INCREMENTAL-TRANSCRIPT against targeted automated tests in extension/src/sidepanel/reasoningTimeline.test.ts, reasoningTimelineView.test.tsx, and reasoningTimeline.chromeSandbox.test.tsx. Local-only validation executed because live_environment_ready=false; 跳过了真实环境验证. In detached worktree at the reviewed commit, targeted Vitest run passed (46 tests), extension typecheck passed, extension build passed, and run_reality_scanner verified canonical intent IDs ELM-FUNC-EXT-PROJECT-TRANSCRIPT and ELM-FUNC-EXT-RENDER-INCREMENTAL-TRANSCRIPT. Direct run_chrome_sandbox on the TSX test entry failed with ERR_UNKNOWN_FILE_EXTENSION .tsx, treated as sandbox toolchain limitation rather than implementation defect because the sandbox-oriented coverage passed under Vitest.

audit.summary: Architecture compliance audit passed for full-model sidepanel transcript batch TASK-014/TASK-015/TASK-016 on commit 90dc29db55ce82d09cee2a7cb96e488bf7e2433b. Reality remains bounded to ELM-COMP-EXT-SIDEPANEL, stable assistant-node projection and incremental tail rendering behavior match approved design intent, canonical scanner-verifiable test tags now verify intended IDs ELM-FUNC-EXT-PROJECT-TRANSCRIPT and ELM-FUNC-EXT-RENDER-INCREMENTAL-TRANSCRIPT, and targeted Vitest coverage passed including adversarial mixed-order and DOM-boundary assertions. Direct run_chrome_sandbox on the TSX test still fails with ERR_UNKNOWN_FILE_EXTENSION .tsx, treated as a tooling limitation rather than an implementation compliance defect because the sandbox-oriented DOM-boundary assertions execute successfully under Vitest.
